text.title: Container Security Scanning and Image Signing Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules for the container scan + SBOM + sign config. Each rule maps to a CI-checkable invariant.

rule.statement: The Trivy (or equivalent) scan step MUST run BEFORE the `docker push` / registry-push step. Push MUST be conditional on scan exit-code 0.

rationale.source: Trivy CI docs, CNCF security WG

rule.rationale: Pushing a vulnerable image to a registry makes it available for deploy. Blocking before push prevents bad images from ever entering the artefact store.

rule.statement: Scanner config MUST emit exit-code 1 on CRITICAL OR HIGH findings. MEDIUM and LOW are informational only at start.
rule.rationale: Blocking on every severity collapses the signal-to-noise ratio; the team starts ignoring CI. CRITICAL+HIGH is the universally accepted hard gate.

rule.statement: Every build MUST emit a CycloneDX SBOM and attach it as an immutable build artefact (release asset / OCI attachment). Do NOT mix CycloneDX and SPDX in the same pipeline.
rule.rationale: Format mixing breaks downstream consumers (Dependency-Track, SIEM). Immutable storage ensures the SBOM is the source of truth for the deployed image bytes.

rule.statement: Image signing MUST use Sigstore cosign keyless OIDC. Cosign private keys MUST NOT live in repo, secrets, or any rotated-by-hand store.
rule.rationale: Keyless signing relies on the CI provider's OIDC identity + Rekor transparency log — no key to rotate, leak, or lose. Stored keys are an audit liability and an attacker target.

rule.statement: Scanner action MUST be pinned to a release tag or SHA. `@master` / `@latest` are forbidden in CI.
rule.rationale: Floating tags mean rule-pack updates silently flip previously-passing builds to failing. CI must be deterministic.

rule.statement: If the Applies If preconditions in AGENTS.md do not all hold, OR any Skip If trigger fires, the agent MUST skip this methodology and pick a different one — applying the rules here on a mismatched input produces wrong output.
rule.rationale: Methodology selection errors cascade. The cheapest place to abort is before any rule fires.
